pattern: Anchoring on each point and grouping the others by their reduced slope counts collinear points without floating-point error.
hints: Any line through the most points must pass through at least one of the points, so fix one point at a time.
Relative to the fixed point, two other points are collinear with it exactly when they share the same direction; represent that direction as a reduced fraction, not a float.
For each anchor, hash the slope of every other point as a normalized (dy, dx) pair divided by their gcd with a consistent sign, take the largest bucket, and add the anchor itself.

edge_inputs.description: three points on the main diagonal
best line holds four of six points
two parallel lines of equal size

stress.description: random points plus a planted collinear run, forcing slope bucketing
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 300))
    pts = set()
    line_len = min(n // 2, 40)
    a = rng.randrange(-5, 6)
    b = rng.randrange(-50, 51)
    x = -line_len
    while len(pts) < line_len and x <= line_len:
        y = a * x + b
        if -10000 <= y <= 10000:
            pts.add((x, y))
        x += 1
    while len(pts) < n:
        p = (rng.randrange(-10000, 10001), rng.randrange(-10000, 10001))
        pts.add(p)
    out = [list(p) for p in pts]
    rng.shuffle(out)
    return [out]
